subject: Human Resources
title: HR Metrics and Analytics
description: A quick, practical guide to HR Metrics and Analytics.

hook.text: A company's turnover rate is 35% but nobody measures it. Leadership blames 'the market' for hiring problems.

buildup.text: HR metrics track measurable data: turnover, time-to-hire, cost-per-hire, engagement scores, and absenteeism.

discovery.text: Analytics turns that data into insights—predicting who might leave, which teams struggle, and where to invest.

twist.text: Data without action is useless. The value comes from using insights to change policies and practices.

climax.text: Data-driven HR makes better decisions, earns executive trust, and proves its impact in dollars.

punchline.text: Measure it, or you can't manage it.

quiz.question: What is the value of HR analytics?
options: Collecting data for reports nobody reads
